mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near multi-decade lows (below 1%) keeps the equity risk premium compressed relative to bond yields, sustaining capital rotation into equities and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as long as the low-rate regime persists.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during periods with FEDFUNDS < 1% are not significantly higher than the unconditional sample mean (t-test p > 0.05), or rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration that historically precedes earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month once the threshold is crossed.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over the trailing 3 months
failure_condition: Following triggered instances, SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or rank IC conditional on trigger is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signals a broad-based consumer demand slowdown that single-series readings understate, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter because both discretionary consumption and durable-goods investment channels weaken together.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS 3-month growth turns negative and HOUST declines more than 5% over 3 months simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1047.0 thousand units in latest snapshot
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following joint-decline signals are not significantly more negative than returns following single-series declines alone (p > 0.05), or rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production readings signal expanding manufacturing capacity utilization during an early-cycle recovery, boosting corporate earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month; this relationship is specific to recovery phases and does not hold in mature expansions.
failure_condition: During periods independently classified as post-shock recovery, INDPRO increases do not correspond to positive 20-day forward returns at rank IC above 0.03, or the effect reverses sign in more than one out of three recovery episodes tested.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects ongoing labor demand strength that supports consumer income and near-term spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following trading week as markets price in continued economic momentum.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 144952.0 thousand persons in latest snapshot
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS prints are not statistically distinguishable from returns following negative prints (t-test p > 0.05), or rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below the trailing year-over-year CPI inflation rate, real policy rates turn negative, incentivizing risk-asset allocation over cash and bonds; the joint condition of low nominal rates and persistent inflation produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter, an effect not captured by either series alone.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation rate is negative
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.4% and CPIAUCSL at 241.002 in latest snapshot
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns during negative real-rate periods are not significantly higher than during positive real-rate periods (p > 0.05), or rank IC on the joint signal is below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts of more than 10% over three months signals an abrupt contraction in interest-rate-sensitive construction activity that leads broader economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following trading week as markets react quickly to the housing data release.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines more than 10% over trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1047.0 thousand units in latest snapshot
failure_condition: Following triggered HOUST declines, SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or rank IC conditional on trigger is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production occurring during a late-cycle expansion regime signals overcapacity and impending margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter; this relationship is regime-specific and does not hold during early-cycle recoveries where output declines reflect temporary restocking adjustments.
failure_condition: During periods independently classified as late-cycle expansion, INDPRO declines do not correspond to significantly negative 60-day forward returns (p > 0.05), or rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
